---
title: "TB Preventive Treatment Cascade — Descriptive Report"
subtitle: "Vladimir, Kovrov, and Murom (Vladimir Oblast, Russian Federation)"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
jupyter: tb-cascade
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

<!--
Phase 6 (Implementation Plan §Phase 6). Mirrors Descriptive Study
Plan.md §7 (Steps 1-10) and §9 (visualization plan) section-for-section,
and `scripts/run_cascade.py`'s data-loading logic, so this report stays
auditable against both documents line by line.

Item 2 ("wire report sections to cascade.py/trends.py/viz.py") is done:
every Step section below calls the real Phase 4 (`cascade.py`/
`trends.py`) and Phase 5 (`viz.py`) functions directly -- no numbers are
hand-typed into this file. Where Phase 5 has a dedicated chart/table
renderer (Steps 1, 2, 6, 9, 10) it is used; where it does not (Steps 3,
4, 5, 7, 8) the step's table is shown as a markdown table via this
file's own `show_table`/`show_tableone` helpers, the same convention
`scripts/run_cascade.py` already uses for its markdown report.

Item 4 (self-describing header) is also done: "Report metadata" prints
the analysis date, the data snapshot's own last-modified date, the
pipeline's git commit hash (`+dirty` if the working tree has
uncommitted changes), and record count, then shows every Sec 6 QC
rule's row counts in/out (`qc.run_qc(raw).summary` -- aggregate counts
only, never the record-level `flagged` table).

Parameters follow Quarto's Jupyter/Papermill convention: override at
render time with, e.g.
    quarto render report/descriptive_report.qmd -P as_of:2026-07-01
-->

```{python}
#| label: parameters
#| tags: [parameters]

as_of: str = "2026-06-16"  # analysis date: Step 8 maturity + incidence-rate person-time
analysis_ready_path: str = ""  # path to analysis_ready_<date>.parquet; "" = rebuild from raw
window_months: int = 12  # right-censoring window (months) when rebuilding from raw
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

from tb_cascade import cascade, derive, trends, viz
from tb_cascade.config import ROOT_DIR, RAW_DATA_PATH
from tb_cascade.io import load_raw

# `raw` is always loaded (a plain CSV read, cheap at this data scale)
# even when `analysis_ready_path` is given, since the "report-header"
# cell's QC row counts (Phase 6 item 4) run against the raw input per
# `scripts/run_qc.py`'s own convention -- a persisted analysis-ready
# Parquet has already been through `derive.build_analysis_table` and is
# not what Sec 6's QC rules are meant to check.
raw = load_raw()
if analysis_ready_path:
    df = pd.read_parquet(analysis_ready_path)
else:
    df = derive.build_analysis_table(raw, as_of, window_months=window_months)

n_records = len(df)


def show_table(df: pd.DataFrame, note: str | None = None) -> None:
    """Render a DataFrame as a markdown table, optionally preceded by an
    italic note -- renders consistently across both the html and gfm
    render targets, mirroring `scripts/run_cascade.py`'s `_section`/
    `_df_md` convention. Used for every step's table that has no
    dedicated `viz.py` renderer (Steps 3, 4, 5, 7, 8)."""
    if note:
        display(Markdown(f"_{note}_"))
    display(Markdown(df.to_markdown(index=False)))


def show_tableone(table1) -> None:
    """Render a `tableone.TableOne` via its own pretty-printed `__repr__`
    inside a fixed-width code block, mirroring `scripts/run_cascade.py`'s
    `_tableone_block` -- used only as a fallback if `viz.baseline_table`
    is unavailable for some reason; Step 1 below uses `viz.baseline_table`
    directly instead."""
    display(Markdown("```\n" + str(table1) + "\n```"))
```

## Report metadata

```{python}
#| label: report-header

import subprocess
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

from tb_cascade import qc


def _git_commit_hash(root: Path) -> str:
    """Short hash of the pipeline commit that produced this report, with
    a `+dirty` suffix if the working tree has uncommitted changes -- so
    a report is never silently attributed to a commit it doesn't
    actually match. Falls back to "unknown" outside a git checkout
    (e.g. git not installed, or a packaged release with no .git dir)."""
    try:
        commit = subprocess.run(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"],
            cwd=root, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
        ).stdout.strip()
        dirty = bool(
            subprocess.run(
                ["git", "status", "--porcelain"],
                cwd=root, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
            ).stdout.strip()
        )
        return commit + ("+dirty" if dirty else "")
    except Exception:
        return "unknown"


def _data_snapshot_date(path: Path) -> str:
    """Last-modified date of the file this run actually loaded data
    from -- the registry export's own snapshot date, not today's date,
    since this report can be re-rendered later against the same data."""
    mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    return f"{mtime:%Y-%m-%d} ({path.name})"


# QC row counts in/out of each Sec 6 rule, run against the raw input
# (see the load-data cell's note) -- `summary` only (rule/n_checked/
# n_violations/violation_rate), never `flagged` (which carries Source/
# Nomer linkage keys and is not safe for this report, per Sec 11).
qc_summary = qc.run_qc(raw).summary

snapshot_source = Path(analysis_ready_path) if analysis_ready_path else RAW_DATA_PATH

print(f"Analysis date (as_of): {as_of}")
print(f"Data snapshot: {_data_snapshot_date(snapshot_source)}")
print(f"Pipeline commit: {_git_commit_hash(ROOT_DIR)}")
print(f"Records (n): {n_records}")
```

```{python}
#| label: report-header-qc

show_table(
    qc_summary,
    note=(
        "QC rule row counts (Descriptive Study Plan §6): `n_checked` is the "
        "denominator the rule applied to (excludes not-applicable records), "
        "`n_violations` is how many of those failed it. Run against the raw "
        "input prior to any derived-variable transforms; see "
        "`reports/qc_report.md` for the full per-site QC appendix."
    ),
)
```

All counts and percentages below have small cells (n < 5) suppressed per
Descriptive Study Plan §11. No row-level identifiers (`Source_id`, `Nomer`,
`IndexCase`) appear in this report — `Source` site-name labels appear only
as an aggregate stratification dimension.

## Step 1 — Population profile (Table 1)

Frequencies/percentages of `Source`, `TargetGroup`, `Sex`, age band, and
`RelationWithSource` (contacts), overall and by site; median/IQR for age
and contacts screened per `IndexCase`.

```{python}
#| label: step1

step1 = cascade.step1_baseline_table(df)
step1_figs = viz.baseline_table(step1)
display(step1_figs["table1"])
display(step1_figs["age_summary"])
display(step1_figs["contacts_per_index_case"])
```

## Step 2 — Screening cascade

Proportion screened → suspected TB → Diaskintest positive → fully
evaluated, for the full cohort and by `TargetGroup`/site.

```{python}
#| label: step2

step2 = cascade.step2_screening_cascade(df)
display(viz.funnel_chart(step2))  # overall — Study Plan §9 bullet 1

step2_by_group = cascade.step2_screening_cascade(df, group_by=["TargetGroup"])
display(viz.funnel_chart_by_group(step2_by_group, group_col="TargetGroup"))

step2_by_site = cascade.step2_screening_cascade(df, group_by=["Source"])
display(viz.funnel_chart_by_group(step2_by_site, group_col="Source"))
```

## Step 3 — Diagnostic outcomes

Among those fully evaluated: proportion `ConfirmedDiagnosisTB`, `LTI`,
`NoTBNoLTI`, `NoTBLTIunknown`; cross-tabulated by `TargetGroup` and site.

```{python}
#| label: step3

step3 = cascade.step3_diagnostic_outcomes(df)
show_table(step3)

step3_by_group = cascade.step3_diagnostic_outcomes(df, group_by=["TargetGroup"])
show_table(step3_by_group, note="By target group")

step3_by_site = cascade.step3_diagnostic_outcomes(df, group_by=["Source"])
show_table(step3_by_site, note="By site")
```

## Step 4 — LTI preventive treatment cascade

Among those eligible: recommended → prescribed → started; initiation
delay (days, median/IQR) and proportion initiated within 30/60 days.

```{python}
#| label: step4

# step4["cascade"]'s stage labels ("recommended"/"prescribed"/"started")
# don't match step2's _STEP2_STAGES, so viz.funnel_chart can't be reused
# here (it validates the stage set) -- shown as a table instead.
step4 = cascade.step4_lti_cascade(df)
show_table(step4["cascade"], note="Cascade stages (% of those eligible for LTI treatment)")
show_table(
    step4["initiation_delay"],
    note="Diagnosis-to-treatment-start delay (days), median/IQR",
)
show_table(step4["initiated_within_target"], note="Initiated within target window")
```

## Step 5 — Regimen description

Among those started on treatment: bedaquiline-containing (`RegBq`) vs.
moxifloxacin-containing (`RegMfx`) regimens, by site and by year of
treatment start.

```{python}
#| label: step5

step5 = cascade.step5_regimen_description(df)
show_table(step5)

step5_by_year = cascade.step5_regimen_description(df, group_by=["Source", "treat_start_year"])
show_table(step5_by_year, note="By site and year of treatment start")
```

## Step 6 — Adherence and completion

Distribution of dose ratio; proportion reaching 50%/100% dose thresholds;
outcome composition (`TreatmentCompleted`/`TreatmentFinished`/non-completion
categories) as a stacked bar summing to 100% of those started.

```{python}
#| label: step6

step6 = cascade.step6_adherence_completion(df)
show_table(step6["adherence_summary"], note="Adherence ratio (doses taken / scheduled), median/IQR")
show_table(step6["dose_threshold"], note="Dose thresholds reached")
display(viz.outcome_stacked_bar(step6["outcome_distribution"]))  # overall

# Study Plan §9 bullet 2: stacked bar of outcome composition by site/target group
step6_by_site = cascade.step6_adherence_completion(df, group_by=["Source"])
display(viz.outcome_stacked_bar(step6_by_site["outcome_distribution"], group_by=["Source"]))

step6_by_group = cascade.step6_adherence_completion(df, group_by=["TargetGroup"])
display(viz.outcome_stacked_bar(step6_by_group["outcome_distribution"], group_by=["TargetGroup"]))
```

## Step 7 — Incentive payment uptake

Proportion receiving each incentive among those eligible, and median delay
between milestone and payment date; compared by site.

```{python}
#| label: step7

step7 = cascade.step7_incentive_uptake(df)
show_table(step7["uptake"], note="Uptake by incentive (% of each incentive's own eligible cohort)")
show_table(
    step7["screening_payment_delay"],
    note="Screening payment delay (days), median/IQR",
)

step7_by_site = cascade.step7_incentive_uptake(df, group_by=["Source"])
show_table(step7_by_site["uptake"], note="Uptake by incentive, by site")
```

## Step 8 — Follow-up and final outcomes

Re-screening at 1 year/24 months; `FinalOutcome` distribution overall and
by treatment completion; TB incidence rate (per 100 person-years) among
LTI starters.

```{python}
#| label: step8

step8 = cascade.step8_followup_outcomes(df, as_of)
show_table(step8["rescreened_1yr"], note="Re-screened at 1 year (among the 1-year-mature cohort)")
show_table(step8["no_tb_after_1yr"], note="No TB after 1 year")
show_table(step8["rescreened_24mo"], note="Re-screened at 24 months (among the 24-month-mature cohort)")
show_table(step8["no_tb_after_24mo"], note="No TB after 24 months")
show_table(step8["final_outcome_distribution"], note="Final outcome distribution")
show_table(
    step8["final_outcome_by_completion"],
    note="Final outcome, stratified by whether preventive treatment was completed",
)
show_table(
    step8["incidence_rate"],
    note="TB incidence rate (per 100 person-years, among LTI starters)",
)
```

## Step 9 — Temporal trends

Enrollment, treatment initiation, and outcome counts by calendar
year/quarter (2018-2026).

```{python}
#| label: step9

step9 = trends.step9_quarterly_trends(df)
display(viz.trend_lines(step9))
```

## Step 10 — Site comparison

Side-by-side summary (Vladimir / Kovrov / Murom) of all cascade
proportions from Steps 2-8 — descriptive flagging only, not a formal
between-site hypothesis test.

```{python}
#| label: step10

step10 = cascade.step10_site_comparison(df, as_of)
step10_figs = viz.site_comparison_table(step10)

# step10_figs mirrors step10's own shape (plain Figure or nested dict of
# Figures); display every leaf in the same order viz._STEP10_TITLES defines.
for value in step10_figs.values():
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        for fig in value.values():
            display(fig)
    else:
        display(value)
```

## Limitations

Per Descriptive Study Plan §10: right-censoring (records enrolled close to
the analysis date cannot yet show a mature outcome); programmatic,
non-randomized data (site/target-group differences may reflect
implementation rather than treatment effects — no causal inference is
intended); structural (not data-quality) missingness for fields that only
apply to certain target groups; outcome ascertainment depends on
follow-up completeness, which may vary by site.
